titleStmt.title: Letter from Maria Edgeworth to Sir Walter Scott, July 6, 1824
titleStmt.author: Maria Edgeworth
titleStmt.sponsor: Texas A & M University, University of Tennessee, Wake Forest University, Xavier University of Louisiana, Southern Methodist University
titleStmt.principal: Susan Egenolf, Meredith Hale, Hilary Havens, Jessica Richard, and Robin Runia

respStmt.resp: photos include: Edgeworth_NLS_3899_1824_07_06_page01.jpg, Edgeworth_NLS_3899_1824_07_06_page02.jpg, Edgeworth_NLS_3899_1824_07_06_page03.jpg, Edgeworth_NLS_3899_1824_07_06_page04.jpg

availability.p: Reproduced by courtesy of the National Library of Scotland
availability.licence: Distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) Unported License

msDesc.head:
Letter from
Maria Edgeworth 1768-1849 71477273 62567029
to Sir Walter Scott, , July 6.th1824

editorialDecl.p: Edgeworth’s spelling and punctuation are retained in transcription, including non-standard spelling, line breaks, and hyphenation of words. The long s and ligatured forms are silently emended.

div.p:
The enclosed specimen of Irish vituperation is warranted genuine by the gentleman who gave it to me - He declares that he wrote it down word word for word as he heard it and I declare that I be- lieve he could not have altered it if he would - and that he would not if he could - There are "some "things I think I could "have made better" - But even in my infinite conceit I respect the truth of Na ture & leave it all to your Honor's honor- I doubted whether Paddy's land was a ge nuine expression, not
having happened to hear it in our parts - & I could have wished to have substituted - the[underline][unclear][/unclear]King[/underline] [underline]doms[/underline] - or the [underline]United[/underline] [underline]Kingdom[/underline] - or [underline]dear little[/underline] [underline]Dublin[/underline] - or in the last [deletion][/deletion] extreme [underline]Dunleary[/underline] [underline]- itself! [/underline]- which according to my notions would have sounded more natural - But upon strict inquiry - (and I have not [underline]spared diligence[/underline] as D[superscript]r[/superscript]. Johnson would boast) I have satisfied myself that Paddy's Land is the true reading - or the true speaking - and I have restored the original - sub mitting contrary to my Irish nature to a Scottish proverb - [underline]Let well alone[/underline] - A proverb
[LibNote]4[/LibNote] I first heard from Joanna Baillie - I have just been reading Reginald Dalton for the second time - with very great pleasure - to some friends to whom it was new [insertion]The story of this novel is so constructed that[/insertion]- The charac ter of Macdonald the father is admirable - There [insertion]just[/insertion] is a depth of knowledge is a depth of knowledge of human nature and exquisite humor - The author probably would shrink & writhe at the idea of trusting it to the reading of an Irish woman but give my services to him and assure him that My Macdonald was heard with unbound ed applause - Like Irish [unclear]Macklin[/unclear] of yore I ruled (not swore) that it [underline]should[/underline] be Scotch, whatever it was There was [insertion]in the author of Reg Dalton[/insertion] great boldness curiosity is kept up & baffled till the last
- the boldness of a mind se cure of original genius, in daring this character after Sir Pertinax McSycophant - It recalls Sir Partinax [underline]only[/underline] by the excellence of the drawing of the master strokes, while it is open essential ly different, & a perfectly new character - It is in beautiful & natural contrast with the amiable vicar- Tell me how your dear Sophia does and little Johnny - our Sophy is now at Cheltenham with her beloved & has her sister Franny with her - in a pretty cottage trellised with roses which she writes me word puts her in mind of Chiefs Wood - her words are "How little I thought when "I was at dear Chiefs Wood "last year that I should "myself so soon be enjoying the same sort of happiness Pray write & tell me how you are all going on - I have had a great loss since I wrote to you last - my sister [unclear]Bedo[/unclear]
